differential_diagnoses: Hemobilia secondary to hepatic artery pseudoaneurysm or hepaticojejunostomy-related vascular injury
Portal hypertensive biliopathy with ectopic varices (e.g., biliary or small bowel varices)
Arteriovenous fistula involving the hepatic vasculature
Portal vein thrombosis with portal hypertension and segmental enteropathy
next_steps: Selective hepatic artery angiography with potential embolization
Deep enteroscopy (balloon-assisted) to visualize the site of the hepaticojejunostomy and rule out occult small bowel sources
Endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography (ERCP) or percutaneous transhepatic cholangiography (PTC) to evaluate the biliary system for hemobilia and malignancy
EUS-guided fine-needle aspiration of the suspicious perihilar hepatic mass/lesion
Liver biopsy if imaging remains indeterminate for malignancy
Multidisciplinary review by interventional radiology, hepatobiliary surgery, and gastroenterology
